what_it_does: Defines a self-contained 1600x900 HTML/CSS composition showing three near-equal-lightness plates labeled Warm, Neutral, and Cool, with metric figures, fragment captions, blueprint corner underlays, a thin hatched footer, and a small V mark.
observations: Imports Plus Jakarta Sans and /surface/styles/vars.css for typography and design tokens.
Uses fixed 1600x900 canvas dimensions with absolutely positioned title, plate grid, footer band, and mark.
Builds three equal grid plates with distinct warm, neutral, and cool backgrounds and comparable internal structure.
The warm plate uses a gold metric figure while neutral and cool plates use charcoal metric figures.
Blueprint grid textures sit in the top-left and top-right corners with radial masks and low opacity.
A later CSS correction overrides the footer band into a quiet 28px hatched strip, hides its original copy, and lifts the V mark.
standouts: Color temperature is presented as three aligned casts at shared visual scale.
The footer keeps a grounding texture while suppressing explanatory text.
The file uses comments as design constraints, including warmth, neutrality, and avoiding blue.
conventions: Uses design-token variables for spacing, type, radius, and colors.
Follows a single-page static HTML composition pattern with embedded CSS.
Uses title case for visible headings and plate labels.
Breaks from clean cascade by using later !important overrides for the footer correction.
concerns: --warm is used but not defined inside this file, so the plate depends on the imported token sheet.
Several footer styles are defined and then overridden later with !important, leaving inactive styling and hidden markup in place.
The canvas is fixed at 1600x900 with no responsive behavior in this file.
notes: A static design substrate comparing warm, neutral, and cool color plates at a shared presentation scale.
questions: Should the hidden footer text remain in the markup after the v2.1 correction?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose of showing warm, neutral, and cool plates at near-equal lightness matches the rendered structure and visible content.
intention_signals: It seems intended to demonstrate how warm, neutral, and cool casts can occupy equivalent visual weight while assigning emphasis, support, and reference roles.
novelty: The file combines a three-plate color-cast study with blueprint corner texture and a deliberately minimized hatched footer correction.
contribution: Contributes a static hue-trio comparison pattern with equalized plate structure, token-driven warm-neutral styling, subtle blueprint underlay texture, and a reduced footer grounding band.
summary_for_embedding: A 1600x900 static HTML/CSS design composition presenting Warm, Neutral, and Cool plates with metrics, captions, blueprint corner grids, and a thin hatched footer.
intention_for_embedding: This file was for exploring three color casts at one lightness level while keeping the composition warm-neutral, structured, and minimally annotated.
